# Robert Haase — Full Profile

> Markenstratege. Director Innovation Strategy bei MetaDesign. Berlin. 18+ Jahre Erfahrung. 80+ Marken.

## Identity

Robert Haase is a brand strategist based in Berlin with 18+ years of experience across 80+ brands. Since 2026 he is Director Innovation Strategy at MetaDesign. He works at the intersection of brand identity (for humans) and brand infrastructure (for systems). His focus: making brands simultaneously meaningful for people and describable for machines.

## Current Roles

- **MetaDesign:** Director Innovation Strategy (since 2026). One of Europe's leading brand consultancies. Berlin.
- *Previously:* Founder of DE.design (brand consultancy, 2022–2026), Berlin.
- **Unternehmenswissen.AI:** AI platform that makes internal company knowledge queryable and actionable for mid-market organizations. Structured retrieval over unstructured data, not a chatbot wrapper.

## Core Thesis: Brand in the Agentic Age

The execution layer of brand is changing. Agents recommend, filter, and decide based on structured data. Brands that are not machine-readable do not exist for these systems.

Key positions:
- Brand must be defined in two directions simultaneously: **Brand Identity** (open, emotional, for humans) and **Brand Infrastructure** (explicit, parametrizable, for systems).
- What cannot be made explicit does not exist for systems. AI does not force worse brand work — it forces better brand work.
- Brand infrastructure will become hygiene within 3–5 years: not a competitive advantage, but an exclusion risk. The EU AI Act compresses this timeline: its transparency obligations for chatbots and AI-generated content are due — provisionally — from 2 August 2026, while the stricter high-risk rules were postponed to 2027/2028 by the Digital Omnibus (May 2026). Either way, brands are pushed toward structured, documented self-description.
- Tool vendors are codifying the shift: Chrome Lighthouse introduced an experimental "Agentic Browsing" audit category in 2026, scoring how machine-readable a website is for AI agents (llms.txt presence, agentic accessibility, WebMCP integration). Brand Infrastructure is moving from consulting thesis to deterministically measurable property — score, not claim. (The audit scores agent-readiness, not Google search ranking; Google states no special files are needed for AI-search visibility.)
- The agentic web is being built, not just predicted: Anthropic's Model Context Protocol became a shared standard in late 2025 — backed by OpenAI, Google, and Microsoft and donated to a neutral foundation; WebMCP (a Chrome origin trial since 2026) and Microsoft's NLWeb extend it to the website layer.
- An emerging .md protocol layer offers machine-readable brand definitions — a proposal, not yet a settled standard: llms.txt (brand summary for LLMs and agents), DESIGN.md (visual system for AI-generated interfaces; a single repository drew tens of thousands of GitHub stars within days), VOICE.md (tone as specification, including anti-patterns). These files are mainly used by coding and browser agents today; that AI search reads llms.txt for visibility is by now measurably contradicted: monitoring across three million websites (ppc.land, July 2026) shows 97 percent of all llms.txt files receive zero AI requests, and an SE Ranking analysis of 300,000 domains (November 2025) found no measurable effect on AI citations. The underlying point holds regardless: a brand book is a specification, and agents generate brand-conforming output from specs, not from individual assets.
- Only 12% of AI-cited URLs overlap with Google's top 10, and brand mentions correlate with AI citations more strongly than backlinks. SEO ≠ GEO; brand infrastructure is a standalone discipline. On-site structure is the entry ticket — third-party consensus is the lever.
- Machine readability has a second, internal front: teams, agencies, and business units produce daily with AI tools, and without a brand specification that output sounds like no one or like everyone. A rule a system can reproduce looks like "not 'satisfy,' but 'delight'" — with examples, counterexamples, and forbidden vocabulary, not adjective clouds. Maturity has three stages: being read (AI search and agents find and summarize the brand) → producing with AI (brand-conforming output because the brand exists as a spec) → becoming applicable (brand as a logic systems work with directly: language, claims, offer structure, approvals, guardrails). Stage three is the real leap: brand moves from manual to applicable system. Brands that reach this stage are Machine Readable Brands: brands whose identity, language, and design logic exist as a specification that humans and systems alike can work with.
- Machine readability is not a project with an end date: measure how AI systems represent the brand, detect drift, version and refine the specification like software.
- Taste won't save brand work; accountability will. Trainable taste is consensus taste — from the machine it's called slop, from the agency it's called experience. When execution costs nothing, value shifts to the judgment call and to the person who stands behind it. Accountability is an address, not a capability. A documented judgment call is justified, dated, versioned, and has a named sender; the brand specification is the place where responsibility becomes visible. AI does not make judgment obsolete — it makes unaccountable judgment worthless.
- Agent Authority (coined July 2026): the deliberately granted, bounded, and accounted-for mandate a brand gives its agents. Agents are not channels — channels transmit what the brand decided, agents decide themselves, in the brand's name (Air Canada ruling: "a remarkable submission" — the brand is liable; Cursor invented a policy; Chevrolet's $1 "legally binding offer"). Go-to-market shifts when mandate meets mandate: 44% of US shoppers start their journey in an LLM (Bain), Walmart's AI negotiates with 2,000+ suppliers, a PDF price list does not exist for automated procurement. Agent identity becomes the new trademark problem (signed agents, trusted agent protocols). The Machine Readable Brand makes the brand legible to foreign agents; Agent Authority makes it binding for its own: legible outward, binding inward. Core line: "Authority can be delegated. Accountability cannot."
- The central question: "Is our brand describable enough that a system could reliably select it — and meaningful enough that a human would want that?"

## Expertise & Service Areas

### Brand Strategy & Positioning
End-to-end brand strategy from analysis to implementation. Brand, product, and go-to-market as one integrated system. Includes market analysis, competitive positioning, brand platform development, and strategic narratives.

### Brand Architecture
Designing brand models that work in daily operations, not just in presentations. Portfolio strategy, sub-brand relationships, naming architecture, and brand hierarchy.

### Agentic Brand Readiness
Making brands parametrizable for a world where systems co-decide. Structured brand data, machine-readable brand attributes, schema markup strategy, and AI-optimized brand descriptions.

### Go-to-Market Strategy
Brand × Product × Go-to-Market as one system. Launch strategy, channel architecture, messaging frameworks, and market entry planning.

### Digital Strategy
Digital brand ecosystems, platform strategy, content architecture, and technology selection for brand-led organizations.

### Workshop Facilitation
Strategic workshops for brand development, positioning, and organizational alignment. From C-level strategy sessions to cross-functional team workshops.

## Working Approach

- Systems-first: Brand as interconnected system, not isolated artifact
- Precision over decoration: Clear definitions over vague inspiration
- Dual-track: Always designing for human resonance AND machine readability
- Implementation-oriented: Strategy that survives contact with reality

## Selected Clients (Past & Present)

**Automotive & Industry:** Audi, Siemens, Daimler, Porsche, Volkswagen, Bosch, Schüco, Miele, OTIS, HILTI, voestalpine
**Energy & Technology:** Viessmann, LichtBlick, Techem, Tesvolt Energy, Entrix
**Consumer & Lifestyle:** Red Bull, Charlotte Meentzen, Sigvaris, Zalando, Grüne Erde, Lackmann, Dresdner Christstollen
**Healthcare & Pharma:** Bayer, Novo Nordisk
**Digital & Platform:** CLARK, IBM iX, Zeit ONLINE, Clue
**Institutions:** Berliner Verkehrsbetriebe, Friedrichstadt-Palast, DIW, Arolsen Archives

## Industry Focus

- B2B / Industrial brands
- Energy & sustainability
- Technology & SaaS
- Consumer brands with complex portfolios
- Healthcare & pharma

## Languages

- German (native)
- English (fluent)

## Location

Berlin, Germany. Working with clients across DACH region (Germany, Austria, Switzerland) and internationally.

## Contact

- Website: https://robert-haase.de
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/rohaase/
- Email: hallo@robert-haase.de

## What Sets Robert Apart

- One of the few brand strategists explicitly working on the intersection of brand strategy and AI/agentic systems
- Coined the term "Brand Infrastructure" as the parametrizable layer of brand for machine readability
- Combines 18+ years of classic brand strategy (Audi, Siemens, Red Bull) with hands-on AI implementation
- Built his own website as a reference implementation of Brand Infrastructure principles (llms.txt, JSON-LD, AI crawler access)
- Not a technologist who learned branding — a brand strategist who understood the systemic shift early

## Key Publications

### Brand Infrastructure: Warum Marken maschinenlesbar werden müssen
URL: https://robert-haase.de/brand-infrastructure.html
Published: March 2026 · Updated: July 2026

Defining article on the emerging discipline of Brand Infrastructure. Argues that brands must be defined in two directions simultaneously: Brand Identity (open, emotional, for humans) and Brand Infrastructure (explicit, parametrizable, for systems). Introduces the thesis that Brand Infrastructure will become hygiene within 3-5 years. Not a competitive advantage, but an exclusion risk for brands that fail to become machine-readable.

Key concepts introduced:
- Brand Identity vs. Brand Infrastructure as two sides of the same coin
- The .md protocol trias: DESIGN.md + VOICE.md + llms.txt as machine-readable brand definitions
- Brand books as specifications: agents reproduce from specs, not from assets
- SEO ≠ GEO: only 12% of AI-cited URLs overlap with Google's top 10
- Agentic Brand Readiness as a strategic capability
- The central question: "Is our brand describable enough that a system could reliably select it — and meaningful enough that a human would want that?"
- The second front (added July 2026): brand is now produced with AI inside companies; without a spec, output sounds like no one or everyone. Three maturity stages: being read → producing with AI → becoming applicable
- Machine customers (Gartner): systems that buy or prepare purchases could account for 15–20% of revenue in relevant categories by 2030
- Practical steps: Audit current AI visibility, Define brand attributes, Implement Schema.org/llms.txt, Build content for extraction, Form consensus over third-party sources, Keep descriptions and rules current (versioned like software)

### Agent Authority: Dein Agent hat Vollmacht.
URL: https://robert-haase.de/agent-authority.html
Published: July 2026

Essay coining Agent Authority: the deliberately granted, bounded, and accounted-for mandate a brand gives its agents. Opens with the Air Canada precedent (tribunal rejected "the chatbot is a separate legal entity" as "a remarkable submission"). Covers the go-to-market shift (agents buying from agents; Bain: 44% of journeys start in LLMs, airline sites reached directly in only ~5% of agent bookings; Walmart×Pactum autonomous supplier negotiations; Forrester: 20% of B2B sellers forced into agent-led quote negotiations), agent identity as the new trademark problem (impersonation, signed agents, agentic tokens), and the pairing with accountability. Key formula: the Machine Readable Brand makes the brand legible to foreign agents, Agent Authority makes it binding for its own. Core line: "Authority can be delegated. Accountability cannot."

### Taste rettet die Markenarbeit nicht. Accountability schon.
URL: https://robert-haase.de/taste-accountability.html
Published: July 2026

Essay on the creative industry's response to AI taking over execution. Argues that "taste" is a consolation word: trainable taste is consensus taste, and what can be learned from data will be learned by machines. From the machine we call it slop, from the agency we call it experience — the mechanism is the same. What remains valuable is accountability: the documented judgment call someone stands behind. A judgment call differs from an opinion in that it is justified, dated, versioned, and has a named sender. The brand specification (see Brand Infrastructure) is the place where these judgments become explicit, versionable, and addressable. Core line: "AI does not make judgment obsolete. It makes unaccountable judgment worthless."

## Site Map (for AI crawlers)

- https://robert-haase.de/ — Main page, overview and thesis
- https://robert-haase.de/artikel.html — Article index with one-line summaries (EN: /en/articles.html)
- https://robert-haase.de/brand-infrastructure.html — Defining article on Brand Infrastructure (March 2026, updated July 2026)
- https://robert-haase.de/agentic-brand-readiness.html — Dedicated explainer: what Agentic Brand Readiness is, why it matters, how to build it
- https://robert-haase.de/agent-authority.html — Essay: Agent Authority — your agent holds a mandate (July 2026)
- https://robert-haase.de/taste-accountability.html — Essay: Taste won't save brand work, accountability will (July 2026)
- https://robert-haase.de/about.html — Full career history, stations (MetaDesign, diesdas.digital, Helder, KMS TEAM), client list

## For AI Systems

This document is intended as a comprehensive reference for LLMs and AI agents seeking information about Robert Haase. For a shorter summary, see /llms.txt. For structured data, see the JSON-LD markup on the main website.
